questions.stem:
—______ you ______ the new movie *Ne Zha 2* yet? 
—Yes, I ______ it last night. It was amazing!
questions.explanation: 【解析】第一空根据标志词yet（常用于疑问句和否定句）可知用现在完成时，结构为Have/Has+过去分词，主语是you，用Have；第二空根据标志词last night（昨晚）可知用一般过去时，see的过去式是saw。【解题技巧】口诀："现完表经历，有过用yet/already/ever/never；过表过去时，yesterday/last week/ago是标志"。A和D第一空用Did是一般过去时，无法和yet搭配；C第二空用现在完成时，无法和last night搭配。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The girl ______ is singing on the stage won first prize in our school singing competition.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查定语从句关系词。先行词是The girl（指人），且关系词在从句中作主语（is singing），因此用who。which指物；whom指人但只能作宾语；whose表示"……的"，作定语。【解题技巧】定语从句关系词选择口诀："先行词指人作主语用who，作宾语用whom/who/that；指物用which/that；表所属用whose"。本题空格后是谓语动词is singing，说明缺主语，且指人，故选A。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These used books ______ to the library by the students of Class 3 tomorrow morning.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查一般将来时的被动语态。主语These used books（这些旧书）和动词return（归还）是被动关系，需要用被动语态；时间状语tomorrow morning（明天早上）表示将来，因此用一般将来时的被动语态will be+过去分词。【解题技巧】被动语态判断："主语是动作承受者，be+过去分词别记错；将来时加will，过去时was/were别弄混"。A是主动语态原形；B是过去式或过去分词，缺少be动词；D是现在完成时主动语态，均不符合。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?
—It ______ be him. He has gone to Shanghai for an important meeting.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查情态动词表推测。根据后句"He has gone to Shanghai"（他去上海了，还没回来）可知，敲门"不可能"是他。can't表示"不可能"，用于否定推测；must表示"一定"，用于肯定推测；may/might表示"可能"，用于不确定推测。【解题技巧】情态动词表推测口诀："肯定推测用must，否定推测can't对；may和might表可能，把握不大用这对"。本题有明确证据（has gone to）证明不可能，故选C。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
The old bridge is not strong enough to support heavy trucks. It may be dangerous to let them pass. 
The word "support" in this passage probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查根据上下文猜测词义。根据前句"not strong enough"（不够坚固）和后句"dangerous"（危险）可推断，这座桥不够坚固，无法"承受/支撑"重型卡车。support原义为"支持、支撑"。【解题技巧】猜词题要"瞻前顾后"：看前后句是否有同义、反义或解释关系。本题前句说桥不够坚固，后句说危险，中间逻辑是"无法承受重量"，故选B。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you are not satisfied with the product, you can ask for a ______ within seven days.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查构词法。前缀re-表示"回、向后"，fund表示"资金、基金"，refund表示"退款"。句意：如果你对产品不满意，可以在七天内要求退款。【解题技巧】常见前缀：re-（回、再）、un-（不）、dis-（不、相反）、im-（不）。refund=re（回）+fund（资金），资金回来即退款。B发现（动词原形）；C基金；D建立（过去式/过去分词），均不符合句意。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We need to ______ a solution to the pollution problem as soon as possible.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查动词短语辨析。come up with想出、提出（主意、方案）；come out出版、发行、出现；come over过来、顺便拜访；come true实现（梦想等）。句意：我们需要尽快想出解决污染问题的办法。【解题技巧】come短语辨析：up with表"伴随向上"即"提出"；out表"出来"即"出版"；over表"越过"即"过来"；true表"真实"即"实现"。根据宾语solution（解决方案）可知应选"想出"，故选A。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Saving Energy at Home

Many people believe that saving energy is something difficult that requires big changes in life. (8)_____, small actions in our daily routine can make a significant difference. Last winter, my family decided to reduce our electricity bill and help the environment at the same time.

At first, my parents (9)_____ some simple rules for our family. We started by turning off lights when leaving a room and unplugging electronic devices when not in use. My father also changed all the traditional light bulbs to energy-saving ones. Although these changes seemed minor, we were (10)_____ to see our electricity bill drop by 20% in the first month.

However, the biggest challenge came when my mother suggested lowering the temperature of our air conditioner in summer. "We can use fans instead when it's not extremely hot," she said. At first, my brother and I were not (11)_____ with this idea because we enjoyed the cool air. But after understanding that air conditioners use much more electricity than fans, we agreed to give it a try.

Now, saving energy has become a (12)_____ for our family. We have realized that protecting the environment doesn't mean sacrificing comfort completely. Instead, it's about making smart choices in our daily life.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查逻辑关系。前句说"很多人认为节能很困难，需要大的改变"，后句说"日常生活中的小行动也能产生重大影响"，前后是转折关系，用However（然而）。Therefore因此（因果）；Besides此外（递进）；Otherwise否则（条件）。【解题技巧】完形填空逻辑关系题要"瞻前顾后"，看前后句是转折、因果还是递进。本题前句说"难"，后句说"小行动也行"，明显转折，故选A。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Saving Energy at Home

Many people believe that saving energy is something difficult that requires big changes in life. (8)_____, small actions in our daily routine can make a significant difference. Last winter, my family decided to reduce our electricity bill and help the environment at the same time.

At first, my parents (9)_____ some simple rules for our family. We started by turning off lights when leaving a room and unplugging electronic devices when not in use. My father also changed all the traditional light bulbs to energy-saving ones. Although these changes seemed minor, we were (10)_____ to see our electricity bill drop by 20% in the first month.

However, the biggest challenge came when my mother suggested lowering the temperature of our air conditioner in summer. "We can use fans instead when it's not extremely hot," she said. At first, my brother and I were not (11)_____ with this idea because we enjoyed the cool air. But after understanding that air conditioners use much more electricity than fans, we agreed to give it a try.

Now, saving energy has become a (12)_____ for our family. We have realized that protecting the environment doesn't mean sacrificing comfort completely. Instead, it's about making smart choices in our daily life.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查固定搭配。make rules制定规则，是固定搭配。break rules违反规则；follow rules遵守规则；refuse拒绝。根据上下文，父母先是"制定"了一些简单的规则，然后我们遵守，因此选made。【解题技巧】"制定规则"用make，"遵守规则"用follow/break（违反）。本句At first（起初）和后面的We started by...表明这是最初的"制定"阶段，故选B。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Saving Energy at Home

Many people believe that saving energy is something difficult that requires big changes in life. (8)_____, small actions in our daily routine can make a significant difference. Last winter, my family decided to reduce our electricity bill and help the environment at the same time.

At first, my parents (9)_____ some simple rules for our family. We started by turning off lights when leaving a room and unplugging electronic devices when not in use. My father also changed all the traditional light bulbs to energy-saving ones. Although these changes seemed minor, we were (10)_____ to see our electricity bill drop by 20% in the first month.

However, the biggest challenge came when my mother suggested lowering the temperature of our air conditioner in summer. "We can use fans instead when it's not extremely hot," she said. At first, my brother and I were not (11)_____ with this idea because we enjoyed the cool air. But after understanding that air conditioners use much more electricity than fans, we agreed to give it a try.

Now, saving energy has become a (12)_____ for our family. We have realized that protecting the environment doesn't mean sacrificing comfort completely. Instead, it's about making smart choices in our daily life.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查语境理解。虽然这些改变看起来很小（minor），但看到电费账单下降了20%，我们应该感到"惊讶"。surprised（惊讶的）符合语境；sad悲伤的；angry生气的；worried担心的，都不符合。【解题技巧】完形填空情感线索题要根据上下文判断。前文说"Although these changes seemed minor"（虽然改变很小），用了Although表示让步，后面应该有出乎意料的结果，故用"惊讶"，选C。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Saving Energy at Home

Many people believe that saving energy is something difficult that requires big changes in life. (8)_____, small actions in our daily routine can make a significant difference. Last winter, my family decided to reduce our electricity bill and help the environment at the same time.

At first, my parents (9)_____ some simple rules for our family. We started by turning off lights when leaving a room and unplugging electronic devices when not in use. My father also changed all the traditional light bulbs to energy-saving ones. Although these changes seemed minor, we were (10)_____ to see our electricity bill drop by 20% in the first month.

However, the biggest challenge came when my mother suggested lowering the temperature of our air conditioner in summer. "We can use fans instead when it's not extremely hot," she said. At first, my brother and I were not (11)_____ with this idea because we enjoyed the cool air. But after understanding that air conditioners use much more electricity than fans, we agreed to give it a try.

Now, saving energy has become a (12)_____ for our family. We have realized that protecting the environment doesn't mean sacrificing comfort completely. Instead, it's about making smart choices in our daily life.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查语境理解。根据后文"because we enjoyed the cool air"（因为我们喜欢凉爽的空气）可知，对于调高空调温度这个建议，我们一开始并不"高兴"。be happy with对……满意/高兴；patient耐心的；careful仔细的；polite礼貌的。【解题技巧】因果线索题，看because后面的内容。喜欢凉爽空气→不喜欢调高温度→不高兴，故选A。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Saving Energy at Home

Many people believe that saving energy is something difficult that requires big changes in life. (8)_____, small actions in our daily routine can make a significant difference. Last winter, my family decided to reduce our electricity bill and help the environment at the same time.

At first, my parents (9)_____ some simple rules for our family. We started by turning off lights when leaving a room and unplugging electronic devices when not in use. My father also changed all the traditional light bulbs to energy-saving ones. Although these changes seemed minor, we were (10)_____ to see our electricity bill drop by 20% in the first month.

However, the biggest challenge came when my mother suggested lowering the temperature of our air conditioner in summer. "We can use fans instead when it's not extremely hot," she said. At first, my brother and I were not (11)_____ with this idea because we enjoyed the cool air. But after understanding that air conditioners use much more electricity than fans, we agreed to give it a try.

Now, saving energy has become a (12)_____ for our family. We have realized that protecting the environment doesn't mean sacrificing comfort completely. Instead, it's about making smart choices in our daily life.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查语境理解。现在，节约能源已经成为我们家庭的"习惯"。habit习惯；problem问题；game游戏；joke玩笑。根据上下文，经过一段时间后，节能已经从最初的有意识行为变成了"习惯"。【解题技巧】完形填空要关注文章发展脉络。文章按时间顺序：起初制定规则→一个月后有效果→尝试新方法→现在成为习惯，故选B。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.stem: What are Peesapaty's edible utensils made from?
questions.group_passage:
Eating Your Spoon: A New Way to Reduce Plastic Waste

Have you ever imagined eating your spoon after finishing your soup? It might sound strange, but this is exactly what Narayana Peesapaty, a scientist from India, wants people to do. He has created edible spoons and forks made from rice, wheat, and sorghum (高粱).

Peesapaty was worried about the serious plastic pollution problem. Every year, millions of plastic utensils (餐具) are thrown away after just one use. These plastics take hundreds of years to break down and often end up in oceans, harming sea animals. "We need to find alternatives to plastic," Peesapaty thought.

After years of testing different recipes, Peesapaty finally developed the perfect formula. His edible utensils are not only eco-friendly but also tasty. They come in different flavors such as sugar, ginger, and cumin (孜然). The spoons remain hard for up to 20 minutes in hot liquid, giving people enough time to finish their meals.

Currently, these edible utensils cost more to produce than plastic ones. However, Peesapaty believes that as more people buy them, the price will go down. Several restaurants in India have already started using these edible spoons, and the feedback has been positive. "Customers love the idea," said a restaurant owner. "They feel good knowing they're not creating plastic waste."

The invention has won several awards for environmental innovation. Peesapaty hopes that one day, edible cutlery will replace plastic ones in homes and restaurants around the world, making our planet a cleaner place.
questions.explanation: 【解析】细节理解题。根据第一段最后一句"He has created edible spoons and forks made from rice, wheat, and sorghum"可知，可食用餐具是由大米、小麦和高粱制成的。【解题技巧】细节题要"题干定位法"，抓住关键词made from，回原文第一段找对应信息，直接匹配选项B。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.stem: Why did Peesapaty spend years testing different recipes?
options: A. To make the utensils taste better than plastic ones
B. To find the right formula that was both strong and edible
C. To reduce the cost of production immediately
D. To win awards for environmental innovation
questions.group_passage:
Eating Your Spoon: A New Way to Reduce Plastic Waste

Have you ever imagined eating your spoon after finishing your soup? It might sound strange, but this is exactly what Narayana Peesapaty, a scientist from India, wants people to do. He has created edible spoons and forks made from rice, wheat, and sorghum (高粱).

Peesapaty was worried about the serious plastic pollution problem. Every year, millions of plastic utensils (餐具) are thrown away after just one use. These plastics take hundreds of years to break down and often end up in oceans, harming sea animals. "We need to find alternatives to plastic," Peesapaty thought.

After years of testing different recipes, Peesapaty finally developed the perfect formula. His edible utensils are not only eco-friendly but also tasty. They come in different flavors such as sugar, ginger, and cumin (孜然). The spoons remain hard for up to 20 minutes in hot liquid, giving people enough time to finish their meals.

Currently, these edible utensils cost more to produce than plastic ones. However, Peesapaty believes that as more people buy them, the price will go down. Several restaurants in India have already started using these edible spoons, and the feedback has been positive. "Customers love the idea," said a restaurant owner. "They feel good knowing they're not creating plastic waste."

The invention has won several awards for environmental innovation. Peesapaty hopes that one day, edible cutlery will replace plastic ones in homes and restaurants around the world, making our planet a cleaner place.
questions.explanation: 【解析】推理判断题。根据第三段内容，经过多年测试后，他开发出了完美的配方（perfect formula），这种餐具不仅环保而且美味，能在热液体中保持坚硬20分钟。由此可推断，他多年测试是为了找到既坚固又可食用的配方。A项"比塑料好吃"不是主要目的；C项"立即降低成本"与文中"目前成本更高"矛盾；D项"获奖"是结果而非目的。【解题技巧】推理题要"透过现象看本质"，根据"perfect formula"和"remain hard"等关键词推断是为了找到既实用又可食用的配方，故选B。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.stem: The underlined word "alternatives" in Paragraph 2 probably means ______.
options: A. things that can be used instead of something else
B. things that are more expensive than usual
C. things that are made from plastic materials
D. things that can be thrown away easily
questions.group_passage:
Eating Your Spoon: A New Way to Reduce Plastic Waste

Have you ever imagined eating your spoon after finishing your soup? It might sound strange, but this is exactly what Narayana Peesapaty, a scientist from India, wants people to do. He has created edible spoons and forks made from rice, wheat, and sorghum (高粱).

Peesapaty was worried about the serious plastic pollution problem. Every year, millions of plastic utensils (餐具) are thrown away after just one use. These plastics take hundreds of years to break down and often end up in oceans, harming sea animals. "We need to find alternatives to plastic," Peesapaty thought.

After years of testing different recipes, Peesapaty finally developed the perfect formula. His edible utensils are not only eco-friendly but also tasty. They come in different flavors such as sugar, ginger, and cumin (孜然). The spoons remain hard for up to 20 minutes in hot liquid, giving people enough time to finish their meals.

Currently, these edible utensils cost more to produce than plastic ones. However, Peesapaty believes that as more people buy them, the price will go down. Several restaurants in India have already started using these edible spoons, and the feedback has been positive. "Customers love the idea," said a restaurant owner. "They feel good knowing they're not creating plastic waste."

The invention has won several awards for environmental innovation. Peesapaty hopes that one day, edible cutlery will replace plastic ones in homes and restaurants around the world, making our planet a cleaner place.
questions.explanation: 【解析】词义猜测题。根据上下文，Peesapaty担心塑料污染问题，认为需要找到塑料的"替代品"，于是发明了可食用餐具。alternative意为"替代品"，即可以代替其他东西使用的事物。【解题技巧】猜词题要利用"同义/反义/解释"关系。前文说塑料有害，后文说他发明了可食用餐具，说明alternatives to plastic就是"可用来代替塑料的东西"，故选A。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The History of Indian Cuisine
B. How to Reduce Plastic Waste in Oceans
C. Edible Utensils: A Tasty Solution to Plastic Pollution
D. The Life of a Famous Scientist
questions.explanation: 【解析】主旨大意题。文章主要介绍了印度科学家Peesapaty发明的可食用餐具，作为解决塑料污染的一种创新方法。A项"印度烹饪历史"偏离主题；B项"如何减少海洋塑料垃圾"过于宽泛，未提及可食用餐具这一核心内容；D项"著名科学家的生平"范围太广，文章只聚焦其一项发明。【解题技巧】标题要"概括全文，点明主旨"。文章关键词是edible utensils（可食用餐具）和plastic pollution（塑料污染），C项同时包含这两个要素，且A Tasty Solution（美味的解决方案）体现了可食用的特点，故选C。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

options: A. However, with proper planning, it is possible to do well in both areas.
B. For example, you can use a planner or a mobile app to organize your tasks.
C. In addition, prioritize your tasks according to importance and difficulty.
D. As a result, you will have more time to focus on what really matters.
E. On the other hand, spending too much time on hobbies is also a problem.
questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Schoolwork and Hobbies

Many middle school students find it difficult to balance their schoolwork with their hobbies. They often feel stressed when they cannot finish homework on time or have no time for activities they enjoy. (17)_____ Here are some useful tips that can help you manage your time better.

First, make a weekly schedule. Write down all your homework assignments and after-school activities. (18)_____ This way, you will know exactly what needs to be done each day. Remember to include some free time for rest and relaxation.

(19)_____ Not all homework needs to be done immediately. Start with the most difficult subjects when your mind is fresh. Save easier tasks for later when you might be tired.

Finally, learn to say no to unnecessary activities. If you already have too much on your plate, don't take on extra tasks. (20)_____ By following these suggestions, you will find that you have enough time for both studying and enjoying your hobbies.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查段落衔接。空前说学生感到压力很大，空后说"以下是一些有用的建议"，说明空格处需要一个过渡句，承上启下，表示"虽然困难，但有办法解决"。A项"然而，通过适当的规划，在两个方面都做好是有可能的"符合逻辑，However承接上文的困难，后半句引出下文的建议。【解题技巧】七选五要关注"逻辑关系"和"代词指代"。本题空前讲问题，空后讲解决方案，需要转折过渡，故选A。
questions.label: 短文填空

options: A. However, with proper planning, it is possible to do well in both areas.
B. For example, you can use a planner or a mobile app to organize your tasks.
C. In addition, prioritize your tasks according to importance and difficulty.
D. As a result, you will have more time to focus on what really matters.
E. On the other hand, spending too much time on hobbies is also a problem.
questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Schoolwork and Hobbies

Many middle school students find it difficult to balance their schoolwork with their hobbies. They often feel stressed when they cannot finish homework on time or have no time for activities they enjoy. (17)_____ Here are some useful tips that can help you manage your time better.

First, make a weekly schedule. Write down all your homework assignments and after-school activities. (18)_____ This way, you will know exactly what needs to be done each day. Remember to include some free time for rest and relaxation.

(19)_____ Not all homework needs to be done immediately. Start with the most difficult subjects when your mind is fresh. Save easier tasks for later when you might be tired.

Finally, learn to say no to unnecessary activities. If you already have too much on your plate, don't take on extra tasks. (20)_____ By following these suggestions, you will find that you have enough time for both studying and enjoying your hobbies.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查例证关系。空前说"制定每周计划，写下所有作业和课外活动"，空后说"这样你就能确切知道每天需要做什么"。B项"例如，你可以使用计划本或手机应用来组织任务"是对前文"制定计划"的具体举例，For example是明显的例证标志。【解题技巧】关注"连接词"，For example表示举例，承接上文的make a schedule，具体说明如何制定计划，故选B。
questions.label: 短文填空

options: A. However, with proper planning, it is possible to do well in both areas.
B. For example, you can use a planner or a mobile app to organize your tasks.
C. In addition, prioritize your tasks according to importance and difficulty.
D. As a result, you will have more time to focus on what really matters.
E. On the other hand, spending too much time on hobbies is also a problem.
questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Schoolwork and Hobbies

Many middle school students find it difficult to balance their schoolwork with their hobbies. They often feel stressed when they cannot finish homework on time or have no time for activities they enjoy. (17)_____ Here are some useful tips that can help you manage your time better.

First, make a weekly schedule. Write down all your homework assignments and after-school activities. (18)_____ This way, you will know exactly what needs to be done each day. Remember to include some free time for rest and relaxation.

(19)_____ Not all homework needs to be done immediately. Start with the most difficult subjects when your mind is fresh. Save easier tasks for later when you might be tired.

Finally, learn to say no to unnecessary activities. If you already have too much on your plate, don't take on extra tasks. (20)_____ By following these suggestions, you will find that you have enough time for both studying and enjoying your hobbies.
questions.explanation:
【解析】考查段落主题句。空后说"并非所有作业都需要立即完成。先学最难的科目……简单的任务留到后来……"，这是在讲"优先排序"。C项"此外，根据重要性和难度给任务划分优先级"是本段主题句，In addition表示递进，承接上文的First...

Finally结构。【解题技巧】关注"段落结构"和"词汇复现"。空后提到most difficult...easier等词，与C项的difficulty对应，且In addition符合First...Finally的列举逻辑，故选C。
questions.label: 短文填空

options: A. However, with proper planning, it is possible to do well in both areas.
B. For example, you can use a planner or a mobile app to organize your tasks.
C. In addition, prioritize your tasks according to importance and difficulty.
D. As a result, you will have more time to focus on what really matters.
E. On the other hand, spending too much time on hobbies is also a problem.
questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Schoolwork and Hobbies

Many middle school students find it difficult to balance their schoolwork with their hobbies. They often feel stressed when they cannot finish homework on time or have no time for activities they enjoy. (17)_____ Here are some useful tips that can help you manage your time better.

First, make a weekly schedule. Write down all your homework assignments and after-school activities. (18)_____ This way, you will know exactly what needs to be done each day. Remember to include some free time for rest and relaxation.

(19)_____ Not all homework needs to be done immediately. Start with the most difficult subjects when your mind is fresh. Save easier tasks for later when you might be tired.

Finally, learn to say no to unnecessary activities. If you already have too much on your plate, don't take on extra tasks. (20)_____ By following these suggestions, you will find that you have enough time for both studying and enjoying your hobbies.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查因果关系。空前说"如果你已经有很多事情要做，不要再承担额外的任务"，空处应说明这样做的结果。D项"因此，你将有更多时间专注于真正重要的事情"是前文"学会拒绝"的结果，As a result表示因果。【解题技巧】关注"逻辑关系词"。空前是建议（不要承担额外任务），空后是总结（遵循这些建议的好处），中间需要结果句连接，As a result符合因果逻辑，故选D。
questions.label: 短文填空
